questions.question: Where was the statue of the Happy Prince placed?
questions.answer: On a tall column above the city.

questions.question: Why had the swallow stayed behind while the other swallows went to Egypt?
questions.answer: Because he had fallen in love with a reed.

questions.question: Why did the swallow decide to stay with the prince for one night?
questions.answer: Because the prince requested him to help the poor seamstress.

questions.question: Why could the Happy Prince not move from his place?
questions.answer: His feet were fixed to the pedestal.

questions.question: What was the seamstress doing when the swallow visited her house?
questions.answer: She was embroidering flowers on a satin gown.

questions.question: What did the swallow do to help the sick boy?
questions.answer: He fanned the boy’s forehead with his wings.

questions.question: Why did the swallow feel warm after helping the poor?
questions.answer: Because he had done a good deed.

questions.question: What problem did the young playwright face?
questions.answer: He was too cold and hungry to finish his play.

questions.question: Why was the swallow unwilling to pluck out the prince’s eye at first?
questions.answer: Because he felt pity for the prince.

questions.question: How did the playwright react when he found the sapphire?
questions.answer: He believed it was a gift from an admirer.

questions.question: Why did the prince ask for his second eye to be given away?
questions.answer: To help a poor match girl.

questions.question: What happened to the match girl’s matches?
questions.answer: They fell in the gutter and were spoiled.

questions.question: Why did the swallow refuse to go to Egypt?
questions.answer: Because he chose to stay with the blind prince.

questions.question: What did the prince ask the swallow to do with the gold leaves?
questions.answer: Give them to the poor people.

questions.question: How did the poor children react after receiving the gold?
questions.answer: They became happy and cheerful.

questions.question: What did the swallow eat during winter?
questions.answer: Crumbs outside the baker’s door.

questions.question: What did the swallow do before dying?
questions.answer: He kissed the prince on the lips.

questions.question: Why did the mayor think the statue was no longer useful?
questions.answer: Because it was no longer beautiful.

questions.question: Why did the councillors agree with the mayor?
questions.answer: Because they always supported him.

questions.question: Why was the statue melted?
questions.answer: Because it had lost its beauty and jewels.

questions.question: Who gave the order to bring the most precious things in the city?
questions.answer: God.

questions.question: Who carried the precious things to God?
questions.answer: An angel.

questions.question: What was one of the precious things chosen by the angel?
questions.answer: The lead heart of the prince.

questions.question: What was the other precious thing chosen?
questions.answer: The dead swallow.

questions.question: What theme does the story highlight?
questions.answer: Kindness and sacrifice.
